blockers: The authoritative verifier failed both GOFS invalid-argument tests with AssertionError, causing :gofs-v1:jvmTest and the build to fail.
The GOFS parameterized methods resolve service.feeds.getValue(...) before their require validation, so a missing feed can become the Result failure instead of the expected IllegalArgumentException.
Using runCatching directly around suspend requests captures CancellationException and may prevent cancellation from propagating.
overall_reasoning: This patch has excellent breadth and repository hygiene. Unlike helper-level wrapping, ordinary public methods execute feed lookup inside runCatching, and all applicable API dump formats are updated. Nevertheless, getWaitTimes and getRealtimeBookings construct URLBuilder from the service feed before checking paired drop-off arguments. The verifier consequently records two failed ResultErrorHandlingTest cases out of three and an authoritative failed outcome. The lack of cancellation rethrowing is an additional safety concern.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: Wrapping each public method makes its exception boundary explicit and avoids the argument-evaluation leak present in helper-only approaches. The repetition is substantial, and cancellation behavior is not made explicit.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: Updating both .api and .klib.api files demonstrates strong awareness of the repository's binary-API tracking, while the source, KDoc, snippets, and tests remain aligned across all modules.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: All requested client modules, public signatures, JVM and klib API declarations, snippets, and producer tests are changed without unrelated work.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Public wrappers reliably contain ordinary lookup, network, HTTP, and parsing exceptions, but indiscriminate runCatching also contains CancellationException. The validation-order bug can additionally report a misleading failure cause.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Existing tests remain useful through getOrThrow adaptations, but no focused tests exercise the newly promised failure semantics. The missing GOFS invalid-argument cases are exactly the two cases the verifier rejects.

strengths: Covers every requested GBFS v1-v3 and GOFS public client method.
Updates both JVM .api and multiplatform .klib.api snapshots for all affected modules.
Places runCatching around complete ordinary public-method bodies, including feed-map lookup, so those methods consistently return lookup failures rather than throwing.
Updates KDoc, documentation snippets, and existing producer tests consistently.
summary: The most repository-complete patch, with both API snapshot formats updated and clear public wrappers, but incorrect GOFS validation ordering and swallowed cancellation prevent merging.
